scores.materiality_reasoning: Portrayal in literature is a status-based resource with minimal direct material payoff.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to the specific domain of poetic representation and characterization.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the duration of the representation/performance of the character.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group defined by sex, a biologically fixed attribute.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes normative poetic standards of his own time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports established artistic decorum, though framed as a prescriptive 'should'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Manliness is not part of the definition of the group 'women'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Moral worth is an abstract, non-material / trait-based resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of social standing and human excellence.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Social/moral standing is a lifelong structural feature of the class.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group defined by sex, which is strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the contemporary social hierarchy of Aristotle's Greece.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports widespread cultural assumptions ('it may be said') as factual social context.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Inferiority is not the definition of woman, but an ascribed status.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Recognition of moral worth is a spiritual/abstract status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational axis of human value within the society.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of a slave's worth is a permanent-till-revoked structural fact.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a legal birth-status, very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary Greek view of the slave class.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Acknowledges common opinion as a factual basis for dramatic theory.
scores.tautology_reasoning: In this context, being a slave is defined as being 'beneath consideration' regarding 'goodness'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Capacity for convincing representation is a professional skill/outcome.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to the technique of poetic composition.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Brief episode; tied to the specific moment of emotional simulation/composition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (poet) is a durable social identity acquired by training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes the methodology of poets in his own era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mostly a theoretical argument/prescriptive advice on how to compose.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Simulating emotion is not the definition of being a poet.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Participation in a recognized public procedure (drama) with tangible outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to the specific institution of the dramatic festival.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource attached to a recurring professional role during festivals.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Role-bound group (chorus members) acting during a specific term/play.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary and historical theatrical practices in Greece.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Argues for a specific aesthetic theory ('it must be') over others.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Dramatic action is not part of the definition of the chorus (singing is).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from social/legal censure (criticism) is a concrete legal/status right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific right related to linguistic standards and professional immunity.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exemption holds across the poet's entire professional career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Poet is an occupation acquired through specialized skill and role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary critical disputes (e.g., Protagoras).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the jurisdictional boundary between arts as an established fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Technical grammar is not part of the definition of poetry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a recognized public practice with material (clarity/fame) consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to specific linguistic procedures in writing.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A permanent-till-revoked professional license for the duration of the career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Professional role (poet) is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Defends current poetic practices against contemporary critics.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports real artistic aid and criticisms, though defends one side.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Word alteration is not how a poet is defined.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a social role practice that enables broad artistic outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scoped to a particular genre of literary institution (epic).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the member participates in that specific genre/vocation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Specialized sub-occupation (epic poet) is a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes structural genre rules in contemporary Greek literature.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes physical/narrative constraints of the stage vs. narrative as self-evident fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Simultaneity is a right afforded by narrative, not its definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to non-standard linguistic professional tools.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific resource; tied to the use of rare words in one metre.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A durable professional license within the chosen genre (epic).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation of epic poet is an identity involving training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the established stylistic norms of the time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the conventions of the hexameter as an observed literary fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Rare words are not the definition of epic poetry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to specific professional tools/roles (metre selection).
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific: limited to the choice of metre in one genre.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The restriction holds throughout the entire creation of the work.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vocation-based identity (epic poet).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes a rigid convention currently active in high culture.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Observes 'no one has composed' as a historical and contemporary fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Metre is the rule, but 'epic' is not defined solely as 'hexameter writer'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from professional standards of other fields (biological accuracy).
scores.generality_reasoning: Exemption spans many technical situations (medicine, biology, etc.).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Professional license valid as long as one produces art.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (poet) is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle defines the contemporary standards for cross-disciplinary errors.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Distinguishes legalistic categories of error in artistic practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Inaccuracy is not the definition of poetry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to represent status-based ideals; symbolic/aesthetic outcome.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to specific creative institutions and artistic procedures.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A durable professional liberty for the life of the career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vocation-bound group (poets, painters).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites Sophocles and Zeuxis as standard models in use.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixed between reporting historical practice and arguing it 'ought to be'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Idealization is not part of the definition of being a poet.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Permission to use non-standard language/diction in public art.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to diction and linguistic modifications.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A durable professional permission recognized by contemporary society.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Vocation-based durable identity (poets).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Explicitly states what we 'allow' in the present.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the societal permission to use such diction as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Rare diction is not the definition of a poet.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to public dignity and protection from vulgar/low-status portrayal.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific standard of decorum spanning theatrical performances.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Class-based dignity is a lifelong structural feature.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by intersection of sex and hereditary nobility.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle critiques 'modern actors' and current stage conventions.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports contemporary critical reaction to breaches of social norms.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Dignity is not the definition of being a noble woman.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Wealth and physical goods (personal effects, furniture).
scores.generality_reasoning: Property rights are a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Property retention/loss has lifelong consequences.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal status birthright, requiring naturalization/exile to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes current Athenian legal procedures regarding confiscation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim cites the factual process of seizure and sale.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Property is not part of the definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Dowry is a physical economic resource (wealth/subsistence).
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational economic axis for women and families in Athens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds for the duration of a marriage and survived death/forfeiture.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group defined by sex, strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The lawyer describes a current legal claim based on Athenian law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the legal obligation ('owed from it') as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A dowry is an asset, not a defining physical trait of a woman.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Tangible wealth and money required to fund state services.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad category of mandatory wealth redistribution (liturgies).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Obligations occur across several years while in the wealth bracket.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a durable status requiring significant effort to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary Athenian system of public liturgy.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Lists specific, verifiable instances of mandatory tax/service.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Compulsory navy funding is not how wealth is defined.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to subsistence and physical rearing (food, shelter).
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad foundational right to family support and care.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the phase of childhood/minority.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age status is biologically fixed at the moment described.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lysias describes a current familial and social obligation in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The 'hardship' assumes the factual necessity of the uncle providing care.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being kin does not mean 'person who pays for rearing' by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical treatment and possession of the body after death.
scores.generality_reasoning: Major recognizable right spanning all citizens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Momentary/one-off: concerns the disposal of the body after death.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lysias discusses the specifics of a contemporary legal execution.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Treats the refusal as a privation, implying the right is standard.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Burial is a legal right, not part of the definition of 'citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Large sums of money (talents, minae) spent on mandatory services.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad category of institutionalized public service costs.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Long-term obligations linked to a career of elite service.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealthy status is durable but not immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Accounts for 50 years of service up to the present moment.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Lists specific factual expenditures to prove loyalty.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Public service funding is an obligation, not the definition of wealth.
